description: Comprehensive Threat Model addressing the 6 primary attack vectors (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege).

threat_matrix.title: Session Hijacking via Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
threat_matrix.description: An attacker dynamically injects malicious JavaScript into the browser to steal standard localStorage JWTs and mathematically spoof the victim's identity.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: JWTs are mathematically locked inside HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax cookies. The browser's JS engine is physically denied read-access to the session token.

threat_matrix.title: API User Enumeration
threat_matrix.description: An attacker sweeps the login API measuring response times or error strings to deduce which Target Emails physically exist.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Error Obfuscation. Fake emails, bad passwords, and physically deactivated accounts identically return HTTP 401 'Incorrect username or password'.

threat_matrix.title: Audit Ledger Database Forgery
threat_matrix.description: A rogue DBA modifies the PostgreSQL logs directly using Superuser credentials to erase evidence of a breach.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Cryptographic Blockchain Hashing. Python recalculates the SHA-256 signature matrices of every row on-the-fly. Tampering mathematically breaks the chain, launching massive UI anomaly alerts.

threat_matrix.title: Malicious Binary Upload
threat_matrix.description: Attacker uploads a malicious executable (e.g., .php or .exe) renamed with a .pdf extension.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Deep Inspection Magic Bytes. The python-magic library ignores extensions and physically tests the OS-level hex headers of the buffer.

threat_matrix.title: Action Denial
threat_matrix.description: A malicious user successfully deletes files or commits actions, then claims they never authorized the transaction.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: The Immutable Ledger strictly logs user_id, Action, and IP Address on all critical events natively.

threat_matrix.title: Encrypted Payload Theft
threat_matrix.description: Hackers compromise the Postgres database disk and dump the tables securely, attempting to read sensitive student Feedback contents.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Fernet AES-GCM Encryption. Feedback text and attachments are encrypted symmetrically natively prior to database writes. Keys are vaulted in isolated RAM environments via .env parameters.

threat_matrix.title: Automated Brute Force / CPU Exhaustion
threat_matrix.description: A botnet unleashes 10,000 requests per second attempting to crack an Admin password, simultaneously consuming all Database connection pools.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: SlowAPI edge routing artificially drops external IPs exceeding 5 requests/minute. The Database natively locks specific target accounts for 60 seconds after 5 failed crypt-hashes.

threat_matrix.title: Vertical Privilege Escalation via Schema Bypass
threat_matrix.description: A standard user sends the API a forged JSON structure injecting {'role': 'admin'} during account creation.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Pydantic strict schema exclusion. The API computationally drops unapproved JSON payloads and manually hardcodes new_user.role = 'user' natively.

threat_matrix.title: Container Execution Escape
threat_matrix.description: A zero-day exploit in FastAPI allows an attacker to execute a reverse-shell inside the Docker container, gaining root host access.
threat_matrix.mitigation_strategy: Principle of Least Privilege. Containers execute inherently via the unprivileged USER directive. docker-compose.yml implements `security_opt: - no-new-privileges:true` enforcing physical kernel blocks against SUID/SGID root escalation.
